title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Automation and Data Client Sy… · Behavior Case: Bearer Token Scope
summary: A scheduled automation rotates API keys and OAuth bearer tokens and isolates cookie jars by account without leaking secrets to notebooks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py bearer token scope increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acquire, attach, scope, refresh, expire, and revoke bearer tokens without treating possession as identity proof. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
